_comment: Full deck-spec schema. The build_deck.py script consumes this. Top-level fields: title, audience, palette, slides[].
title: ProjectX Migration — Board Update

slides.title: ProjectX Migration
slides.subtitle: Board update — Q3 FY26
slides.notes: Set the scene in one sentence — we're 60% through, ask is to approve overrun budget.

slides.title: Where we are
slides.notes: Quick orientation before diving into the numbers.

slides.title: Migration is 60% complete and on track for Q1 close
bullets: 8 of 14 services cut over with zero customer-visible incidents
Performance up 22% on migrated services (p99 latency)
Two services pushed to phase 3 — schema rewrite needed first
slides.notes: Lead with the positive — zero incidents matters. Note the slipped services so it isn't a surprise later.

slides.title: Cost savings already realised
slides.caption: Annualised infra savings from the 8 migrated services
slides.notes: Pause here. Let the number land. Then say: we expect $5.8M annualised at full completion.

slides.title: What's left vs. what's added
slides.notes: Frame the scope additions honestly. Multi-region was a regulator change, not a planning miss.

slides.quote: The migration went smoother than any I've seen in 20 years — and our team learned more in 6 months than the prior 2.
slides.notes: Optional, but lands well with a board that's worried about morale.

slides.title: Top risks for the remaining 40%
bullets: Schema rewrite — 6 weeks, narrowly de-risked by dry runs
Auth refactor coupling — increases coordination overhead
Holiday-period freeze cuts available calendar by 4 weeks
slides.notes: Don't soften the risks. Boards trust presenters who name them.

slides.title: Asking for $620k FY26 budget overrun
slides.subtitle: Covers schema rewrite + multi-region; payback < 8 months
slides.notes: Land on the Ask. Take questions here, not after a thank-you slide.
